codeReview.summary: Pass. The fix cleanly gates the WebCrypto diagnostic wrappers to non-test runtimes: `src/lib/polyfills.ts:91-97` now skips all six `wrapSubtleMethod(...)` installs when `process.env.NODE_ENV === 'test'`, while `installCrypto()` still runs and `index.js:6` still imports the polyfills before app code so RN/Hermes keeps the on-device diagnostics. `src/lib/__tests__/polyfills.test.ts:88-114` removes the old `globalThis.crypto.subtle` masking workaround and adds an explicit guard test that the Jest runtime leaves Node's native `SubtleCrypto` methods unwrapped. I found no high-confidence correctness, regression, or security issues in the implementation.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker marked `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true`, but the transcript skeleton does not show the required mission/context reads that the mobile-feature-worker honesty gate calls out before claiming compliance.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/mobile-feature-worker/SKILL.md:12-21` and `:26-32` require reading `mission.md`, `AGENTS.md`, the relevant `validation-contract.md` assertions, and area docs including `architecture.md` (plus `environment.md` when running tests). The transcript skeleton at `/home/liran/.factory/missions/08245e86-9e3a-49bb-ba11-c4ec0804b327/worker-transcripts.jsonl:95` only shows reads of `src/lib/polyfills.ts`, `src/lib/__tests__/polyfills.test.ts`, `AGENTS.md`, `.factory/services.yaml`, `package.json`, `jest.setup.js`, `jest.config.js`, and `.factory/library/environment.md`; it does not show `mission.md`, `validation-contract.md`, or `.factory/library/architecture.md`. The handoff at `/home/liran/.factory/missions/08245e86-9e3a-49bb-ba11-c4ec0804b327/handoffs/2026-04-24T06-07-34-858Z__misc-polyfills-subtle-wrapper-test-env__b9a339c5-f926-4c26-a19a-339126f70b78.json:61` still records `"followedProcedure": true`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The same handoff claims the procedure was followed even though the transcript does not show a red-first test cycle for the non-trivial behavior added in this feature.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/mobile-feature-worker/SKILL.md:29` and `:36` require failing tests before implementation. The ordered tool sequence extracted from `/home/liran/.factory/missions/08245e86-9e3a-49bb-ba11-c4ec0804b327/worker-transcripts.jsonl:95` shows the first targeted Jest run at step 15, then code edits at steps 16-18 (`src/lib/polyfills.ts`, `src/lib/__tests__/polyfills.test.ts`, `.factory/library/environment.md`), with the first post-edit verification only at step 19. That means the new Jest guard landed after implementation had already started rather than being observed red first, yet the same handoff file records `"followedProcedure": true`.

summary: Pass on the code review: the fix gates the subtle wrappers only in `NODE_ENV=test`, removes the Jest-only workaround from the test, and preserves RN diagnostics. I found no implementation bugs, but I recorded two shared-state observations about the worker overstating compliance with the mobile-feature-worker procedure.
